verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (cysteine-rich secretory protein 1) is secreted by the epididymal epithelium and becomes stably associated with the sperm surface during epididymal transit. Two pools are described: a loosely-bound pool that is removed during capacitation, and a tightly-bound pool that resists washing and remains on the sperm head, where it participates in sperm-egg fusion. This wash-resistant deposition onto the sperm plasma membrane during transit through a specialized secretory compartment fits stable_surface_attachment, and the display is restricted to the male germline lineage. Not a TM/GPI protein itself; reaches the outer leaflet only via post-translational anchoring to sperm.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether the tightly-bound sperm-surface CRISP1 pool is truly wash-resistant covalent/non-covalent anchoring vs. reversible lipid/protein binding in equilibrium.
